Notice Of Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE INC., ATOBRHAN GEBRESLASSIE, and PERVEZ HAYAT'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel is DENIED. Despite the existence of the bankruptcy stay, the court has the jurisdiction to entertain this sanctions motion to vindicate its regulatory powers. (See Keitel v. Heubel, (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 324, 334.) The court concludes that plaintiff violated the bankruptcy stay, but whether a bankruptcy stay applies to a non-debtor co-defendant such as Mr. Gebreslassie is not always clear and A.H. Robins even notes that it is an "unusual situation." (See A.H. Robins Co. Piccinin (1986) 788 F.2d 994, 999.) Here, Plaintiff's counsel's belief that Mr. Gebreslassie may have had separate insurance was a reasonable basis to continue to pursue him. While the prudent approach would have been to seek relief from the bankruptcy court, it cannot be said that plaintiff's conduct was "frivolous" within the meaning of CCP 128.5. Plaintiff is warned to take no further prosecutorial action until he obtains relief from the bankruptcy stay from the bankruptcy court.

Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (L&M302)
